output:
CVE-2024-21797 describes a high-severity command execution vulnerability (CWE-74) found in the adm.cgi set_TR069() function of Wavlink AC3000 firmware version M33A8.V5030.210505. This vulnerability allows an attacker to execute arbitrary commands via specially crafted HTTP requests, given authenticated access.

The core issue arises from improper neutralization of external input, which leads to command execution risks. The associated MITRE ATT&CK Technique is T1190, covering vulnerabilities exploited through inadequate input validation.

Potential impacts include unauthorized access and control over affected devices, notably impacting devices running the specified vulnerable firmware. Mitigation strategies include applying available patches, employing network segmentation, and using intrusion detection systems to monitor and block suspicious activities.
